frameworks.name: fMRI multivariate decoding
frameworks.one_line: decode reach direction from EVC voxel patterns in congenitally blind

frameworks.name: cross-modal plasticity
frameworks.one_line: EVC represents non-visual information after early blindness

frameworks.name: predictive action-visual space alignment
frameworks.one_line: hard-wired mechanism aligns motor reach space with retinotopic map

frameworks.name: dorsal stream visuo-motor integration
frameworks.one_line: dorsal areas critical for visuo-spatial transformation even without sight

core_question: Can reach direction be decoded from early visual cortex of congenitally blind humans without visual experience or imagery?
key_assumptions: fMRI patterns in EVC carry decodable action-related information
congenital blindness eliminates visual experience and visual imagery
foveal vs peripheral retinotopy is preserved in blind EVC
manipulations: EVC subregions along foveal-to-peripheral retinotopic gradient
key_findings: reach direction reliably decoded from EVC fMRI in congenitally blind
decoding accuracy highest in foveal-representing EVC, decreases toward peripheral
decoding also strong in dorsal stream visuo-spatial / visuo-motor areas
interpretations: visual experience and imagery are not necessary for EVC action representation
a predictive hard-wired mechanism may align action space with foveal visual space
